scene.description: Case C-001. The first case you ever worked — your home memory, the one that feels like where you began. It is warm and simple and stable. That stability is the first thing that should worry you.

fragments.label: The First Desk
fragments.text: Your desk, brand new, before the wear on the third drawer. Maya's voice, younger somehow, walking you through your first reconstruction. It felt like being trusted with something.

fragments.label: The Simple Case
fragments.text: An easy one. A misremembered birthday. You found the truth in minutes and felt, for the only time you can recall, that the job was good. That it helped someone.

fragments.label: The Issued Memory
fragments.symbol: You examine it closely and the warmth has a serial number. This 'first case' is standard-issue — every Keeper gets the same one, so every iteration has a home to miss. The comfort is manufactured.

fragments.label: Maya, From the Start
fragments.text: She was here on day one. Every day one. She has done this opening with versions of you more times than the count will hold still for — and she has meant it every time. That is the part that isn't manufactured.

interpretations.label: Keep it as home, manufactured or not
Reject the issued comfort as a leash
